goal: Recommend what this project should borrow from mature procurement systems next while staying on the current stack.
deliverable: A list of current repo defects that matter now
constraints: Ground the answer in this repo rather than generic ERP theory
Use mature products as references, not as a mandate to copy full ERP scope
Do not implement code changes in this turn
Do not reopen Rust as the primary recommendation
acceptance_criteria: The analysis explains which mature procurement patterns are worth borrowing now
The analysis distinguishes present capabilities from missing workflow objects
The recommendation includes both product and engineering hardening priorities
product_acceptance_criteria: The user can see a clearer next step than a rewrite
The recommendation fits the current product stage and stack
manual_spot_checks: Check current item, supplier, inventory, and invoice tables
Check current routing and workflow boundaries
Cross-check against official Odoo and ERPNext procurement docs
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after the analysis is written, repo-grounded, and clearly bounded as analysis-only output.
delivery_truth_contract: State only confirmed repo facts plus explicit engineering judgment; do not imply mature-project features already exist locally.
inferred_assumptions: The user wants to improve the current product rather than change the stack.
The repo is mature enough to benefit from stronger procurement objects and action queues.
A smaller, focused version of mature procurement patterns is more valuable than copying full ERP breadth.
